macro_context.market_sentiment: Mixed-to-cautious following Mag 7 earnings week. Dow -0.57% on Apr 29 (48,862); S&P 500 -0.04% (7,136); Nasdaq +0.04% (24,673). Tech earnings broadly beat but META tumbled ~7-9% AH on capex raise, while Alphabet surged on Google Cloud AI strength. Market absorbed the Fed hold with limited reaction.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC voted 8-4 to hold rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 — third consecutive hold. Unprecedented 4-way dissent. Inflation elevated partly due to global energy prices. Most participants see rates staying 3.25%-3.75% through end of 2026. Kevin Warsh advancing as next Fed chair, replacing Powell.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict is the dominant risk: oil spike (+19% energy commodities forecast), potential Strait of Hormuz disruption. Trump preparing extended Iran blockade (WSJ). US-Venezuela tensions and NATO posturing in Greenland add uncertainty. IMF global growth cut to 3.1%, headline inflation at 4.4%. Tariff uncertainty persists.
key_events: Fed rate decision already released Apr 29 — next FOMC meeting ~June 2026
Kevin Warsh Senate confirmation vote pending (Senate Banking Committee already advanced nomination)
Iran blockade decision by Trump administration could spike oil further
US midterm elections later in 2026 — political risk premium building
Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon Q1 2026 earnings all reported this week alongside META

ticker_news.headline: Meta Q1 2026 EPS $10.44 (vs $6.65 est) and revenue $56.31B (+33% YoY) beat expectations — but shares fell ~7% AH on capex raise
Meta raises full-year 2026 capex guidance to $125B-$145B (from $115B-$135B) for AI infrastructure buildout
Meta daily active people (DAP) of 3.56B — up 4% YoY but down >5% QoQ, with 'internet disruptions in Iran' cited
Meta laying off ~8,000 employees (10% of workforce) while freezing 6,000 open roles to offset rising capex
Meta debuts Muse Spark, its first proprietary foundation AI model, following $14B Alexandr Wang deal

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Rothschild & Co upgraded from Neutral to Buy on Jan 26, 2026. Consensus of 38-78 analysts is Strong Buy (28 buy, 5 hold, 0 sell per one source). Price target range $676-$1,144 with median ~$840-$856. Post-Q1 earnings some analysts issued Hold ratings in $700 range citing capex overhang, but majority remain bullish. Source: MarketBeat / StockAnalysis / TipRanks (Apr 29, 2026)

web_context.competitor_activity: Alphabet (Google) surged after strong Google Cloud AI revenue, demonstrating AI monetization ahead of Meta — a bearish signal for Meta's AI ROI narrative. OpenAI and Anthropic continue to outpace Meta in foundation models, which pressured reaction to Muse Spark launch. Microsoft reported solid earnings. Snap's stalled growth gives Meta an opening with its new 'Instants' disappearing-photo app. Amazon reported alongside Meta with strong AWS numbers, reinforcing cloud/AI spending themes.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 just reported April 29 AH: EPS $10.44 (beat $6.65 est by 57%), Revenue $56.31B (beat $55.45B est). Next earnings Q2 2026 expected late July 2026 — no official date announced yet. Pre-earnings Q1 consensus was EPS ~$6.65-$6.79, Revenue ~$55.36-$55.56B. Source: CNBC / MarketBeat / Meta Investor Relations
